mnemonics.title: MIMD Memory Types: S vs D
mnemonics.content: Shared memory = Simpler to program but limited Scalability. Distributed memory = Difficult to program but excellent Scalability. The trade-off is always ease-of-use vs. scale.

mnemonics.title: MESI Protocol States
mnemonics.content: Modified (dirty, exclusive -- data differs from memory), Exclusive (clean, exclusive -- only copy, matches memory), Shared (clean, shared -- multiple caches have it), Invalid (stale -- not valid data).

mnemonics.title: MIMD = Most Important Modern Design
mnemonics.content: Multi-core CPUs, clusters, cloud servers, supercomputers -- all are MIMD. It is the dominant architecture because it supports both data and task parallelism with maximum flexibility.

mnemonics.title: UMA vs NUMA Analogy
mnemonics.content: UMA = one shared dining table (equal access for all). NUMA = each person has a desk with their own food (fast) but can walk to others' desks (slow). UMA is simpler, NUMA scales better.

mnemonics.title: Shared-Memory Challenges: C.S.M.
mnemonics.content: Cache coherence overhead, Synchronization complexity (locks, barriers), Memory contention (bus bottleneck). These three limit shared-memory MIMD scalability.
